title: Friday Sermon 24 May 2002
description:
Today the Friday Sermon is on the Divine attribute of al-Shaheed (The Observer).  The meaning of the word Shaheed connotes observing something - while being present - with the obvious sight or with discerning insight.*

With the customary reference to Quranic verses and their various commentaries – 

1.  'The Holy Quran' admonishes regarding rights of mankind on each other and that while for the  obedient it is a promise it serves as a warning for the disobedient.

(Fakhruddin al-Razi.  'Tafsir e Kabir: Mafatih al-Ghayb').

2.  All peace and goodness is from Allah and man creates his own misery.  Allah the Exalted  bore witness by fulfilling His prophecy in time by the advent of the Holy Prophet (peace and  blessings of Allah be on him) and Allah also bore witness in that Muhammad (peace and  blessings be on him) was indeed from Him by granting him triumph over his enemies.

(Hazrat al-Hajj Hakeem Maulana Nooruddin – Khalifatul Masih I.  'Haqaiq al-Furqan: ii'.  40).

‘I said nothing to them except that which Thou didst command me — Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.  I was a witness over them as long as I remained among them, but since Thou didst cause me to die Thou hast been the Watcher over them; for Thou art Witness over all things.’

Verse 118 of Surah Al-Maidah ['The Banquet'] conclusively illustrates that Jesus (on whom be peace) died a natural death.  The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that on the Day of Judgment when Allah the Exalted will send some of his Ummah to hell for making innovations in religion the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him)** will reply like Jesus (on whom be peace) had that while he lived he was witness to what his Ummah did but after his death only Allah was a Witness over them:

It was narrated that Ibn Abbas said: The Messenger of Allah stood up to give an admonition and he said ‘O people! You will be gathered to Allah naked.’  One of the narrators Abu Dawud said ‘Barefoot and uncircumcised.’  The narrators  Waki and Wahb said ‘Naked and uncircumcised.  As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it.  The first one to be clothed on the Day of Resurrection will be Abraham, peace be upon him.  Then some men from among my Ummah will be brought and will be taken toward the left.  I will say: O Lord!  My companions.' It will be said 'You do not know what they innovated after you were gone,' and I shall say what the righteous servant said 'I was a witness over them as long as I remained among them, but since Thou didst cause me to die Thou hast been the Watcher over them; and You are a Witness to all things.'  
  
'If Thou punish them they are Thy servants; and if Thou forgive them Thou surely art the Almighty, the Wise.'***
 
It will be said: 'These people kept turning away since you left them.'

(Abdul Rahman Ahmad bin Shuayb al Nasai.  'As-Sunan al-Kubra').
 
The Quranic verse decisively demonstrates and bears witness that Jesus (on whom be peace) is dead and that the verse does not give any testimony about Jesus’ (on whom be peace) second coming.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Aina e kamalat e Islam'.  Riyaz e Hind, 1893.  'Ruhani Khazain: v'.  433).

Nothing is hidden from Allah the Exalted and that He watches over everything great and small.

* Abul Qasim al-Hussayn ibn Mufaddal ibn Muhammad al-Raghib. 'Al Mufradat fi Gharib al-Quran'.
** The day when we shall roll up the heavens like the rolling up of scrolls by a scribe.  As We began the first creation so shall We repeat it – a promise binding upon Us; we shall certainly perform it.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Anbiya [The Prophets]: 105).
* Al Maidah: 119.
